Title: The Joys of Motherhood
Author: Buchi Emecheta
Notes: Powerful and brutal; recommended by Nadia for the Enugu parallel

Title: An Introduction to Plant Biotechnology
Author: H. S. Chawla
Notes: Reference reading for Emeka's Chapter 2 guidance; strong on marker-assisted selection

Title: A Psalm for the Wild-Built
Author: Becky Chambers
Notes: Read in two evenings; exactly what I needed after grant deadline week

Title: Homegoing
Author: Yaa Gyasi
Notes: Stunning; bought impulsively at Standard Book Centre with two others

Title: Plant Breeding and Genetics
Author: Seth Narro et al.
Notes: Useful for yam section; somewhat dated on molecular methods

Title: The Famished Road
Author: Ben Okri
Notes: Dense and worth it; third read; keeps giving

Title: Americanah
Author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
Notes: Reread; every time more layers; read on the drive to the field site

Title: Food Sovereignty and Peasants
Author: La Via Campesina (ed.)
Notes: Essential for the cooperative framing chapter idea; very political

Title: The Linden Tree
Author: Leila Aboulela
Notes: Aboulela speaks directly to the in-between; bought at Houston airport last December

Title: African Smallholder Farming Systems
Author: IFPRI Research Report
Notes: Excellent for grant proposal framing; extensive Nigeria coverage

Title: The Kite Runner
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Notes: Second read; cried again; need not explain

Title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Notes: Back to back Hosseini was a lot but I have no regrets

Title: Plant Molecular Biology
Author: Bob Goldberg
Notes: Checking cassava RNA extraction protocols for Emeka's work

Title: Purple Hibiscus
Author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
Notes: Keep returning to Enugu through her pages

Title: Miniature Wife and Other Stories
Author: Manuel Gonzalez
Notes: Birthday impulse buy; uneven but the title story is great

Title: The Ministry for the Future
Author: Kim Stanley Robinson
Notes: Dense but important; the agriculture chapters hit hard

Title: Tender is the Flesh
Author: Agustina Bazterrica
Notes: Disturbing; makes you think about food systems in a gut-punch way

Title: The Joys of Motherhood
Author: Buchi Emecheta
Notes: Re-read because I loaned the copy to Adaeze; bought a second
